article_title: NEOPRISM-CRC: Nine Weeks of Presurgery Pembrolizumab Leaves All 32 Bowel Cancer Patients Relapse-Free at 33 Months
summary: REJECT: Duplicate of PR #1328 (NEOPRISM-CRC) which was approved and published on 2026-05-19. Same clinical trial, same data, same event.

content_preview.title: NEOPRISM-CRC: Nine Weeks of Presurgery Pembrolizumab Leaves All 32 Bowel Cancer Patients Relapse-Free at 33 Months
content_preview.summary: A Phase II trial replacing post-operative chemotherapy with three cycles of pembrolizumab before surgery shows zero relapses at nearly three years in dMMR/MSI-high colorectal cancer.

recommendations: Close the PR without merging. Duplicate of PR #1328 which has already been approved and published.

editor_notes.source_verification: Not performed — rejected as duplicate. PR #1328 (NEOPRISM-CRC trial) was approved and published on 2026-05-19 covering the identical clinical trial, same patient cohort, same 33-month follow-up result.
editor_notes.originality: DUPLICATE. This submission covers the exact same story as PR #1328 ('Nine Weeks of Immunotherapy Before Surgery Leaves Every Bowel Cancer Patient Relapse-Free After 33 Months in NEOPRISM-CRC Trial'), which was approved and published today. Both cover the NEOPRISM-CRC Phase II trial with 32 patients, 33-month follow-up, zero relapses, pembrolizumab preoperative treatment, presented at AACR 2026.
concerns: Exact duplicate of PR #1328 published at src/content/articles/2026-05/19-nine-weeks-of-immunotherapy-before-surgery-leaves-every-bowel-cancer-patient-relapse-free-after-33-months-in-neoprism-crc-trial.md
recommendations: Do not resubmit — the topic has been covered.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT — duplicate of PR #1328 already published.
